quick_skim.title: Coarse-to-Fine Domain Incremental Learning with Attentive Distillation for Mining Footprint Segmentation in Multispectral Imagery
quick_skim.abstract: Automatically mapping and segmenting global mining footprints using remote sensing and deep learning is critical for monitoring the socio-environmental risks and impacts of mining, yet its progress is hindered by the scarcity of fine-grained annotated data. Although large-scale datasets with coarse boundaries are widely available, leveraging them to improve fine-grained segmentation is challenging due to significant domain shift. To address this, we propose MineC2FNet, a coarse-to-fine domain incremental learning framework that exploits abundant coarse data to enhance fine-grained mining footprint segmentation. MineC2FNet adopts a teacher-student architecture with attentive distillation at both the feature and prediction levels, selectively transferring generalized knowledge from the coarse domain while enabling boundary refinement using limited fine-grained data (fine domain). We further introduce an expertly validated dataset of 219 images with precise boundary annotations across diverse geographies and commodities. Extensive experiments against state-of-the-art approaches, including domain adaptation and domain incremental learning methods, demonstrate that MineC2FNet achieves superior performance while effectively handling domain shift. The dataset and code are publicly available at https://github.com/risqiutama/MineC2FNet.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: remote sensing deep learning segmentation with domain incremental learning
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 遥感深度学习分割与域增量学习
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 遥感深度学习分割与域增量学习
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 遥感深度学习分割与域增量学习
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: MineC2FNet presents a coarse-to-fine domain incremental learning framework for mining footprint segmentation in multispectral imagery, leveraging attentive distillation to transfer knowledge from coarse to fine-grained segmentation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对全球采矿足迹自动分割中精细标注数据稀缺而粗标注数据丰富的现状，提出MineC2FNet，一种基于注意力蒸馏的粗到细域增量学习框架。该框架采用教师-学生架构，利用丰富的粗标注数据提升精细分割性能，并通过注意力蒸馏缓解域偏移。在多个遥感数据集上验证了方法的有效性，为遥感图像分析提供了可迁移的域适应范式。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对全球采矿足迹自动分割中精细标注数据稀缺而粗标注数据丰富的现状，提出MineC2FNet，一种基于注意力蒸馏的粗到细域增量学习框架。该框架采用教师-学生架构，利用丰富的粗标注数据提升精细分割性能，并通过注意力蒸馏缓解域偏移。在多个遥感数据集上验证了方法的有效性，为遥感图像分析提供了可迁移的域适应范式。

quick_skim.title: Dual-Pathway Geometry-Aware MLLM for Spatial Intelligence
quick_skim.abstract: Spatial understanding of the physical world from 2D visual inputs hinges on two complementary forms of geometric knowledge: holistic 3D structural perception and fine-grained metric scale estimation. Existing multimodal large language models (MLLMs) typically address only one facet, ingesting either depth maps or point clouds as additional model inputs, which incurs substantial computational overhead and inherits the generalization limitations of upstream prediction models. We propose GAMSI, a dual-pathway Geometry-Aware MLLM for Spatial Intelligence that takes only RGB images as input while internalizing both forms of geometric prior within a unified autoregressive backbone. Specifically, we introduce Metric-Structure Decoupled Queries (MSDQ) which employ two groups of learnable queries to respectively extract dense metric signals and sparse structural cues from the shared visual context, with a task-decoupled attention mask further preventing the two pathways from contaminating each other. Building on this, an Expert-Guided Visual Grounding (EVG) module projects the aggregated cues back to frame-level visual features and aligns them with vision foundation models, which serve purely as training-time supervision, rather than as model inputs. We further build a multi-task spatial instruction-tuning dataset (MTS) comprising 152{,}776 samples spanning 13 task types and three visual modalities, consolidated from six public datasets. Trained with a two-stage curriculum, GAMSI achieves state-of-the-art performance on seven spatial intelligence benchmarks.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: proposes dual-pathway geometry-aware MLLM for spatial intelligence, relevant to geospatial foundation models
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出双路径几何感知多模态大语言模型用于空间智能，与地理空间基础模型相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出双路径几何感知多模态大语言模型用于空间智能，与地理空间基础模型相关
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出双路径几何感知多模态大语言模型用于空间智能，与地理空间基础模型相关
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes GAMSI, a dual-pathway MLLM that internalizes 3D structure and metric scale from RGB images only, addressing limitations of prior methods that require depth or point clouds.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有MLLM通常需要深度图或点云辅助才能理解三维结构，限制了泛化性并增加计算开销。本文提出GAMSI，一种双路径几何感知MLLM，仅凭RGB图像即可内化3D结构和度量尺度信息。通过度量-结构解耦查询和多任务训练，模型在多个空间推理基准上达到最优。该方法为无需额外传感器输入的一般空间智能提供了新范式。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有MLLM通常需要深度图或点云辅助才能理解三维结构，限制了泛化性并增加计算开销。本文提出GAMSI，一种双路径几何感知MLLM，仅凭RGB图像即可内化3D结构和度量尺度信息。通过度量-结构解耦查询和多任务训练，模型在多个空间推理基准上达到最优。该方法为无需额外传感器输入的一般空间智能提供了新范式。

quick_skim.title: From General Vision to Reliable Traversability Estimation: Adapting Vision Foundation Models for Unstructured Outdoor Environments
quick_skim.abstract: Vision-based approaches have become the dominant paradigm for traversability estimation in unstructured outdoor environments, typically adapting vision foundation models (VFMs) via semantic segmentation supervision. However, this paradigm faces three fundamental challenges that undermine its reliability: the task-agnostic design of VFMs, the ambiguity of traversability annotations, and the discrepancy between semantic labels and physical safety. We propose Vision-to-Traversability Adaptation (ViTA), a framework that adapts VFMs for reliable traversability estimation, instantiated on SAM2. ViTA injects task-specific knowledge through learnable traversability prompts while preserving the VFM's cross-domain generalization. To handle annotation ambiguity, we introduce Perspective-Diversified Training, which estimates semantic uncertainty to suppress confident predictions at ambiguous boundaries. To bridge the semantic-traversability discrepancy, we distill geometric knowledge during training, enabling slope and elevation reasoning from RGB images alone at inference. The semantic and geometric outputs are fused into a continuous traversability score that reflects both semantic uncertainty and geometric risk. Evaluations across diverse domains, including challenging real-world off-road datasets, demonstrate that ViTA achieves state-of-the-art IoU and Precision with substantial false-positive reduction and strong cross-domain generalization.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Adapting vision foundation models for outdoor traversability
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 适配视觉基础模型用于户外穿越性估计
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 适配视觉基础模型用于户外穿越性估计
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 适配视觉基础模型用于户外穿越性估计
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: ViTA adapts vision foundation models (SAM2) for reliable traversability estimation in unstructured outdoor environments via learnable prompts while preserving VFM capabilities.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 视觉基础模型在户外穿越性估计中面临任务无关设计、标注歧义和语义-安全差异等挑战。ViTA框架通过可学习的穿越性提示注入任务知识，同时保留基础模型的通用能力，基于SAM2实现。在多个户外数据集上的实验表明，ViTA显著提升了穿越性估计的可靠性和泛化性，为将通用视觉基础模型应用于地理空间任务提供了有效范式。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 视觉基础模型在户外穿越性估计中面临任务无关设计、标注歧义和语义-安全差异等挑战。ViTA框架通过可学习的穿越性提示注入任务知识，同时保留基础模型的通用能力，基于SAM2实现。在多个户外数据集上的实验表明，ViTA显著提升了穿越性估计的可靠性和泛化性，为将通用视觉基础模型应用于地理空间任务提供了有效范式。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 我想查找有关遥感基础模型的论文, especially work that connects or combines: What are foundation models for remote sensing?; Pretrained models for remote sensing tasks; Foundation models in earth observation; Vision transformers for remote sensing; Geospatial foundation models; papers on foundation models for remote sensing image analysis; large scale pretrained models for remote sensing applications.
